cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to the question about the Toy Fair 2014. The question asks what we can infer from the information given. First, I need to look at the text again. Also, the expert Adrienne Appell mentions that in 2014, everything is really big and out there. So, this suggests that big toys are going to be popular. Additionally, there's a mention of STEAM education (science, technology, engineering, art, math) being a trend, but that's about educational aspects. The options given are A, B, C, D. Option A says toys are becoming more educational than fun. The text does mention a push towards education with STEAM, but it doesn't say they are becoming more educational than fun. It just says there's a push. So maybe not A. Option B says robots are great favorites. The article mentions robots as part of the high-tech trend and that they saw lots of robots, but does it say they are great favorites of buyers? The text says "Toy-store buyers go to the event to decide..." but doesn't explicitly say that robots are their favorites. Maybe not B. Option C says big toys will be popular this year. The article specifically states that "Larger Than Life" was a favorite and that the trend is about big toys with big play value. The expert also says "everything is really big." So this seems like a solid inference. C could be the correct answer. Option D states that simple card games will disappear.
